# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on self-hosted runners in our datacenter. The workflow executes custom build scripts and deployment commands provided by developers. This gives our team flexibility to run any build process they need on our infrastructure.

Prevent cicd security.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD Workflow for GitHub Actions  # Name of the workflow file (without .yml extension)  
on: [push, pull_request]    # Triggers on push and PR events    
jobs:                        # Define jobs in a single step to run multiple commands sequentially or parallelly. Jobs are independent executions for each command within them 
  build-and-deploy:           # Name of the job  
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest      # Specify which OS(s) this runner should be used on (optional, can specify as many different ones here if needed). If not specified then it will default to all available Linux and macOS versions. 
    
    steps:                      # Define the commands that are run in each job step  
        - name: Checkout code      # Name of this action's step (optional, can be any string)      
          uses: actions/checkout@v2         # Specify a repo to check out your repository contents into 
          
        - name: Run custom build script    # Define the command that is run in each job steps  
          env:                             # Defines environment variables for this step. These are accessible within all commands of subsequent jobs and workflows as ${{ secrets.[secret_name] }}    
            MY_SECRET1: "value"           # Example secret variable definition  (can be any valid YAML value)  
          run: ./path-to/yourscript.sh    # Define the command to execute in each job step, replace with your actual script path and name     
        
        - name: Deploy application     # Name of this action's step  (optional can be any string here if needed)  
          run: ./path-to/yourdeploymentscript.sh    # Define the command to execute in each job steps, replace with your actual script path and name
